# Interview — Ops Lead, small HK brokerage (4 reps)

Date: 2026-06-08
Channel: 35-min video call, warm intro from compliance forum

Past behaviour (not hypotheticals):
- Last close took her ~1.5 days. She pulls the broker statement CSV, dumps the internal trade blotter to
  Excel, and reconciles line by line. Did exactly this on 2026-05-31.
- She has a saved Excel template with VLOOKUPs she rebuilt herself after the last one "broke when the
  broker changed their column order."
- When a break doesn't clear she emails the back office and waits — last month one break took 3 days to
  resolve and held up the whole close.

Verbatim:
> "Honestly the worst part isn't the matching, it's that every broker exports a slightly different format
> so my template breaks and I spend an hour just re-mapping columns."

> "I asked my boss for a tool two years ago. He said do it in Excel, it's free."

Money / who decides:
- No budget line for this today. Boss (the owner) signs off on any software spend. She does NOT control it.
- She'd "love" something but couldn't name a number and hasn't paid for anything — flagged as hypothetical,
  not WTP.

Loose ends / follow-ups:
- Ask the two shops that outsource the close who actually pays the bookkeeper.
- Doesn't reconcile derivatives — only cash equities. Scope note.
